title: cytisus malacitanus subsp. catalaunicus
intro: Cytisus malacitanus subsp. catalaunicus, also known as Spanish broom, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, specifically a shrub that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic, and is used to treat various health conditions, including kidney stones, bladder issues, and inflammation. The health benefits of Cytisus malacitanus subsp. catalaunicus include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in herbal remedies, such as tinctures and capsules.
